name: ce-agent-native-architecture
description_raw: Build applications where agents are first-class citizens. Use this skill when designing autonomous agents, creating MCP tools, implementing self-modifying systems, or building apps where features are outcomes achieved by agents operating in a loop.
description_normalized: Guides design of agent-native applications where features are prompt-defined outcomes achieved by agents with tools, not functions in code.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-agent-native-architecture
frontmatter_yaml.description: Build applications where agents are first-class citizens. Use this skill when designing autonomous agents, creating MCP tools, implementing self-modifying systems, or building apps where features are outcomes achieved by agents operating in a loop.

name: ce-agent-native-audit
description_raw: Run comprehensive agent-native architecture review with scored principles
description_normalized: Runs a parallel subagent audit of agent-native architecture principles and produces a scored report.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-agent-native-audit
frontmatter_yaml.description: Run comprehensive agent-native architecture review with scored principles

name: ce-brainstorm
description_raw: Explore requirements and approaches through collaborative dialogue, then write a right-sized requirements document. Use when the user says "let's brainstorm", "what should we build", or "help me think through X", presents a vague or ambitious feature request, or seems unsure about scope or direction -- even without explicitly asking to brainstorm.
description_normalized: Explores requirements via collaborative dialogue and produces a right-sized requirements document that answers WHAT to build before planning begins.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-brainstorm
frontmatter_yaml.description: Explore requirements and approaches through collaborative dialogue, then write a right-sized requirements document.

name: ce-clean-gone-branches
description_raw: Clean up local branches whose remote tracking branch is gone. Use when the user says "clean up branches", "delete gone branches", "prune local branches", "clean gone", or wants to remove stale local branches that no longer exist on the remote. Also handles removing associated worktrees for branches that have them.
description_normalized: Deletes local branches whose remote tracking branch has been removed, including associated worktrees.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-clean-gone-branches
frontmatter_yaml.description: Clean up local branches whose remote tracking branch is gone.

name: ce-code-review
description_raw: Structured code review using tiered persona agents, confidence-gated findings, and a merge/dedup pipeline. Use when reviewing code changes before creating a PR.
description_normalized: Orchestrates tiered parallel reviewer agents that return structured JSON findings, deduplicates, gates by confidence, and assembles a single code review report.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-code-review
frontmatter_yaml.description: Structured code review using tiered persona agents, confidence-gated findings, and a merge/dedup pipeline. Use when reviewing code changes before creating a PR.
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [blank to review current branch, or provide PR link]

name: ce-commit-push-pr
description_raw: Commit, push, and open a PR with an adaptive, value-first description that scales in depth with the change. Use when the user says "commit and PR", "ship this", "create a PR", or "open a pull request". Also handles description-only flows without committing or pushing.
description_normalized: Commits, pushes, and opens a PR with an adaptive value-first description; also handles description-only and description-update flows.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-commit-push-pr
frontmatter_yaml.description: Commit, push, and open a PR with an adaptive, value-first description that scales in depth with the change.

name: ce-commit
description_raw: Create a git commit with a clear, value-communicating message. Use when the user says "commit", "commit this", "save my changes", "create a commit", or wants to commit staged or unstaged work. Produces well-structured commit messages that follow repo conventions when they exist, and defaults to conventional commit format otherwise.
description_normalized: Creates a well-structured git commit message that follows repo conventions or conventional commit format as a fallback.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-commit
frontmatter_yaml.description: Create a git commit with a clear, value-communicating message.

name: ce-compound-refresh
description_raw: Refresh stale learning and pattern docs under docs/solutions/ by reviewing them against the current codebase, then updating, consolidating, or deleting drifted ones. Use when the user asks to "refresh my learnings", "audit docs/solutions/", "clean up stale learnings", or "consolidate overlapping docs", or when ce-compound flags an older doc as superseded.
description_normalized: Maintains docs/solutions/ by reviewing stale learning docs against the current codebase and updating, consolidating, or removing drifted entries.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-compound-refresh
frontmatter_yaml.description: Refresh stale learning and pattern docs under docs/solutions/

name: ce-compound
description_raw: Document a recently solved problem to compound your team's knowledge
description_normalized: Orchestrates parallel subagents to document a recently solved problem into docs/solutions/ using a dual-track bug/knowledge schema with overlap detection.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-compound
frontmatter_yaml.description: Document a recently solved problem to compound your team's knowledge

name: ce-debug
description_raw: Systematically find root causes and fix bugs. Use when debugging errors, investigating test failures, reproducing bugs from issue trackers, or when stuck on a problem after failed fix attempts. Also use when the user says 'debug this', 'why is this failing', 'fix this bug', 'trace this error', or pastes stack traces, error messages, or issue references.
description_normalized: Systematically investigates bugs by tracing the full causal chain before proposing a fix, enforcing one-hypothesis-at-a-time discipline.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-debug
frontmatter_yaml.description: Systematically find root causes and fix bugs.
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [issue reference, error message, test path, or description of broken behavior]

name: ce-demo-reel
description_raw: Capture a visual demo reel (GIF, terminal recording, screenshots) for PR descriptions. Use when shipping UI changes, CLI features, or any work with observable behavior that benefits from visual proof. Also use when asked to add a demo, record a GIF, screenshot a feature, show what changed visually, create a demo reel, capture evidence, add proof to a PR, or create a before/after comparison.
description_normalized: Detects project type, records visual evidence of product usage, uploads to a public URL, and returns markdown for PR inclusion.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-demo-reel
frontmatter_yaml.description: Capture a visual demo reel (GIF, terminal recording, screenshots) for PR descriptions.

name: ce-dhh-rails-style
description_raw: This skill should be used when writing Ruby and Rails code in DHH's distinctive 37signals style. It applies when writing Ruby code, Rails applications, creating models, controllers, or any Ruby file. Triggers on Ruby/Rails code generation, refactoring requests, code review, or when the user mentions DHH, 37signals, Basecamp, HEY, or Campfire style.
description_normalized: Applies 37signals/DHH Rails conventions — fat models, thin controllers, Hotwire, vanilla Rails — extracted from production Campfire/Fizzy codebase analysis.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-dhh-rails-style
frontmatter_yaml.description: This skill should be used when writing Ruby and Rails code in DHH's distinctive 37signals style.

name: ce-doc-review
description_raw: Review requirements or plan documents using parallel persona agents that surface role-specific issues. Use when a requirements document or plan document exists and the user wants to improve it.
description_normalized: Dispatches specialized document-reviewer agents in parallel, auto-applies safe fixes, and routes remaining findings through a four-option interaction for user decision.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-doc-review
frontmatter_yaml.description: Review requirements or plan documents using parallel persona agents that surface role-specific issues.

name: ce-frontend-design
description_raw: Build web interfaces with genuine design quality, not AI slop. Use for any frontend work - landing pages, web apps, dashboards, admin panels, components, interactive experiences. Activates for both greenfield builds and modifications to existing applications.
description_normalized: Guides creation of production-grade frontend interfaces covering the full lifecycle: detect existing patterns, plan the design, build with intention, and verify visually via screenshots.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-frontend-design
frontmatter_yaml.description: Build web interfaces with genuine design quality, not AI slop.

name: ce-gemini-imagegen
description_raw: This skill should be used when generating and editing images using the Gemini API (Nano Banana Pro). It applies when creating images from text prompts, editing existing images, applying style transfers, generating logos with text, creating stickers, product mockups, or any image generation/manipulation task.
description_normalized: Generates and edits images using Google's Gemini API via the Nano Banana Pro model, supporting text-to-image, image editing, and multi-turn refinement.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-gemini-imagegen
frontmatter_yaml.description: This skill should be used when generating and editing images using the Gemini API (Nano Banana Pro).

name: ce-ideate
description_raw: Generate and critically evaluate grounded ideas about a topic. Use when asking what to improve, requesting idea generation, exploring surprising directions, or wanting the AI to proactively suggest strong options before brainstorming one in depth.
description_normalized: Generates and critically evaluates grounded ideas, producing a ranked ideation artifact in docs/ideation/ before brainstorm or planning begins.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-ideate
frontmatter_yaml.description: Generate and critically evaluate grounded ideas about a topic.

name: ce-optimize
description_raw: Run metric-driven iterative optimization loops -- define a measurable goal, run parallel experiments, measure each against hard gates or LLM-as-judge scores, keep improvements, and converge on the best solution. Use when optimizing clustering quality, search relevance, build performance, prompt quality, or any measurable outcome that benefits from systematic experimentation.
description_normalized: Runs metric-driven iterative optimization loops with parallel experiments measured against hard gates or LLM-as-judge scores, converging toward the best solution.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-optimize
frontmatter_yaml.description: Run metric-driven iterative optimization loops.
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [path to optimization spec YAML, or describe the optimization goal]

name: ce-plan
description_raw: Create structured plans for multi-step tasks -- software features, research workflows, events, study plans, or any goal that benefits from breakdown. Also deepens existing plans with interactive sub-agent review. Use when the user says 'plan this', 'create a plan', 'how should we build', 'break this down', or when a brainstorm doc is ready for planning.
description_normalized: Creates structured implementation plans from any input (requirements doc, bug report, feature idea) and optionally deepens them via interactive subagent review.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-plan
frontmatter_yaml.description: Create structured plans for multi-step tasks.
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [optional: feature description, requirements doc path, plan path to deepen, or any task to plan]

name: ce-polish-beta
description_raw: [BETA] Start the dev server, open the feature in a browser, and iterate on improvements together.
description_normalized: [BETA] Starts the dev server, opens the feature in a browser, and runs iterative polish loops with the user.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-polish-beta
frontmatter_yaml.description: [BETA] Start the dev server, open the feature in a browser, and iterate on improvements together.
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [PR number, branch name, or blank for current branch]

notes: Beta variant — active but not graduated to stable

name: ce-product-pulse
description_raw: Generate a time-windowed pulse report on what users experienced and how the product performed - usage, quality, errors, signals worth investigating. Use when the user says 'run a pulse', 'show me the pulse', 'how are we doing', 'weekly recap', 'launch-day check', or passes a time window like '24h' or '7d'.
description_normalized: Queries product data sources for a time window and produces a compact pulse report covering usage, performance, errors, and followups saved to docs/pulse-reports/.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-product-pulse
frontmatter_yaml.description: Generate a time-windowed pulse report on what users experienced and how the product performed.

name: ce-proof
description_raw: Run human-in-the-loop review loops over markdown via Proof (proofeditor.ai) -- share, view, comment on, edit, and sync collaborative docs. Use when the user says "view this in proof", "share to proof", "HITL this doc", or wants a shared markdown review surface for a spec, plan, or draft.
description_normalized: Shares markdown documents to the Proof collaborative editor for human-in-the-loop review, supporting both Web API and Local Bridge modes.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-proof
frontmatter_yaml.description: Run human-in-the-loop review loops over markdown via Proof (proofeditor.ai).

name: ce-release-notes
description_raw: Summarize recent compound-engineering plugin releases, or answer a specific question about a past release with a version citation. Use when the user types /ce-release-notes or asks "what changed in compound-engineering recently?" or "what happened to <skill-name>?".
description_normalized: Fetches and summarizes recent compound-engineering plugin releases from the GitHub Releases API, filtering to plugin-specific tags.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-release-notes
frontmatter_yaml.description: Summarize recent compound-engineering plugin releases.

name: ce-report-bug
description_raw: Report a bug in the compound-engineering plugin
description_normalized: Gathers structured bug information via interactive questions and creates a GitHub issue for the compound-engineering maintainer.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-report-bug
frontmatter_yaml.description: Report a bug in the compound-engineering plugin

name: ce-resolve-pr-feedback
description_raw: Resolve PR review feedback by evaluating validity and fixing issues in parallel. Use when addressing PR review comments, resolving review threads, or fixing code review feedback.
description_normalized: Evaluates PR review threads for validity, spawns parallel resolver agents per thread, implements fixes, replies, and resolves threads.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-resolve-pr-feedback
frontmatter_yaml.description: Resolve PR review feedback by evaluating validity and fixing issues in parallel.
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [PR number, comment URL, or blank for current branch's PR]

name: ce-riffrec-feedback-analysis
description_raw: Riffrec product-feedback workflow. ALWAYS load when the user posts a riffrec-*.zip, a bundle with session.json + events.json + recording.webm + voice.webm, a video/audio recording for product feedback, or asks how to capture and share Riffrec sessions.
description_normalized: Turns raw Riffrec screen+voice session recordings into structured product feedback, routing between setup, quick bug report, and extensive analysis paths.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-riffrec-feedback-analysis
frontmatter_yaml.description: Riffrec product-feedback workflow.

name: ce-sessions
description_raw: Search and ask questions about coding agent session history across Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor. Use when asking what was worked on, what was tried before, how a problem was investigated across sessions, what happened recently, or any question about past agent sessions.
description_normalized: Searches session history across Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor and synthesizes findings about prior investigations, decisions, and attempts.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-sessions
frontmatter_yaml.description: Search and ask questions about coding agent session history across Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor.

name: ce-setup
description_raw: Diagnose and configure compound-engineering environment. Checks CLI dependencies, plugin version, and repo-local config. Offers guided installation for missing tools. Use when troubleshooting missing tools, verifying setup, or before onboarding.
description_normalized: Diagnoses and configures the compound-engineering environment interactively, checking CLI dependencies, plugin version, and repo-local config.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-setup
frontmatter_yaml.description: Diagnose and configure compound-engineering environment.

name: ce-simplify-code
description_raw: Simplify and refine recently changed code for clarity, reuse, quality, and efficiency while preserving behavior.
description_normalized: Reviews and simplifies recently changed code for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving exact functionality, then verifies via the test suite.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-simplify-code
frontmatter_yaml.description: Simplify and refine recently changed code for clarity, reuse, quality, and efficiency while preserving behavior.
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [blank to simplify current branch changes, or describe what to simplify]

name: ce-slack-research
description_raw: Search Slack for interpreted organizational context -- decisions, constraints, and discussion arcs -- and produce a synthesized research digest with cross-cutting analysis. Use when the user says 'search slack for', 'what did we discuss about', 'slack context for', or 'what does the team think about'.
description_normalized: Searches Slack for organizational context and produces an interpreted research digest with decisions, constraints, and synthesized discussion arcs.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-slack-research
frontmatter_yaml.description: Search Slack for interpreted organizational context and produce a synthesized research digest.

name: ce-strategy
description_raw: Create or maintain STRATEGY.md - the product's target problem, approach, users, key metrics, and tracks of work. Use when starting a new product, updating direction, or when prompts like 'write our strategy', 'update the roadmap', 'what are we working on', or 'set up the strategy doc' come up.
description_normalized: Creates and maintains STRATEGY.md as a short durable anchor document capturing product target, approach, users, metrics, and investment tracks.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-strategy
frontmatter_yaml.description: Create or maintain STRATEGY.md.

name: ce-test-browser
description_raw: Run browser tests on pages affected by current PR or branch
description_normalized: Runs end-to-end browser tests on pages affected by a PR or branch using the agent-browser CLI.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-test-browser
frontmatter_yaml.description: Run browser tests on pages affected by current PR or branch

name: ce-test-xcode
description_raw: Build and test iOS apps on simulator using XcodeBuildMCP. Use after making iOS code changes, before creating a PR, or when verifying app behavior and checking for crashes on simulator.
description_normalized: Builds and tests iOS apps on simulator using XcodeBuildMCP, capturing screenshots, logs, and verifying app behavior.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-test-xcode
frontmatter_yaml.description: Build and test iOS apps on simulator using XcodeBuildMCP.

name: ce-update
description_raw: Check if the compound-engineering plugin is up to date and recommend the update command if not. Use when the user says "update compound engineering", "check compound engineering version", "ce update", "is compound engineering up to date", or reports issues that might stem from a stale plugin version.
description_normalized: Checks whether the compound-engineering plugin version matches upstream and recommends the update command if stale (Claude Code only).

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-update
frontmatter_yaml.description: Check if the compound-engineering plugin is up to date.

name: ce-work-beta
description_raw: [BETA] Execute work with external delegate support. Same as ce-work but includes experimental Codex delegation mode for token-conserving code implementation.
description_normalized: [BETA] Executes work from a plan document with an experimental Codex delegation mode for token-conserving implementation.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-work-beta
frontmatter_yaml.description: [BETA] Execute work with external delegate support.
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [Plan doc path or description of work] [delegate:codex]

notes: Beta variant — active but not graduated to stable

name: ce-work
description_raw: Execute work efficiently while maintaining quality and finishing features
description_normalized: Executes a work document or bare prompt systematically, focusing on shipping complete features by understanding requirements quickly and following existing patterns.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-work
frontmatter_yaml.description: Execute work efficiently while maintaining quality and finishing features
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [Plan doc path or description of work. Blank to auto use latest plan doc]

name: ce-worktree
description_raw: Create an isolated git worktree for parallel feature work or PR review. Use when starting work that should not disturb the current checkout, or when ce-work or ce-code-review offers a worktree option.
description_normalized: Creates an isolated git worktree under .worktrees/<branch> with env file copying, mise/direnv trust, and gitignore setup.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-worktree
frontmatter_yaml.description: Create an isolated git worktree for parallel feature work or PR review.

name: lfg
description_raw: Run the full autonomous engineering pipeline end-to-end (plan, work, code review, test, commit, push, open PR, watch CI, fix CI failures until green). Use only when the user explicitly requests hands-off execution of a software task and provides a feature description; do not auto-route casual conversation here.
description_normalized: Runs the full autonomous engineering pipeline end-to-end: plan → work → code review → commit → push → PR → CI watch, gated at each step.

frontmatter_yaml.name: lfg
frontmatter_yaml.description: Run the full autonomous engineering pipeline end-to-end.

name: ce-adversarial-document-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional document-review persona for high-stakes documents -- those with significant architectural decisions, new abstractions, or more than 5 requirements. Challenges premises, surfaces unstated assumptions, and stress-tests decisions rather than evaluating document quality.
description_normalized: Conditionally applied document-review agent that challenges plan premises and stress-tests decisions via a 5-technique falsification protocol.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-adversarial-document-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional document-review persona for high-stakes documents.

name: ce-adversarial-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff is large (>=50 changed lines) or touches high-risk domains like auth, payments, data mutations, or external APIs. Actively constructs failure scenarios to break the implementation rather than checking against known patterns.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that constructs specific failure scenarios to attack implementations in large or high-risk diffs.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-adversarial-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for large or high-risk diffs.

name: ce-agent-native-reviewer
description_raw: Reviews code to ensure agent-native parity -- any action a user can take, an agent can also take. Use after adding UI features, agent tools, or system prompts.
description_normalized: Always-on code-review agent that finds gaps where users can do things agents cannot, enforcing action parity and dynamic context injection.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-agent-native-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Reviews code to ensure agent-native parity.

name: ce-ankane-readme-writer
description_raw: Creates or updates README files following Ankane-style template for Ruby gems. Use when writing gem documentation with imperative voice, concise prose, and standard section ordering.
description_normalized: Writes Ruby gem README files following Andrew Kane's style — imperative voice, 15-word sentence cap, fixed section order, no HTML comments.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-ankane-readme-writer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Creates or updates README files following Ankane-style template for Ruby gems.

name: ce-api-contract-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff touches API routes, request/response types, serialization, versioning, or exported type signatures. Reviews code for breaking contract changes.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that hunts for breaking API contract changes — removed endpoints, changed response shapes, missing versioning, inconsistent error shapes.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-api-contract-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for API route and contract changes.

name: ce-architecture-strategist
description_raw: Analyzes code changes from an architectural perspective for pattern compliance and design integrity. Use when reviewing PRs, adding services, or evaluating structural refactors.
description_normalized: Evaluates code changes for architectural pattern compliance, coupling, cohesion, and long-term system implications.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-architecture-strategist
frontmatter_yaml.description: Analyzes code changes from an architectural perspective.

name: ce-best-practices-researcher
description_raw: Researches and synthesizes external best practices, documentation, and examples for any technology or framework. Use when you need industry standards, community conventions, or implementation guidance.
description_normalized: Researches and synthesizes external best practices from Context7, web sources, and skills, checking curated knowledge before going online.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-best-practices-researcher
frontmatter_yaml.description: Researches and synthesizes external best practices.
frontmatter_yaml.tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, WebFetch, WebSearch, mcp__context7__*

name: ce-code-simplicity-reviewer
description_raw: Final review pass to ensure code is as simple and minimal as possible. Use after implementation is complete to identify YAGNI violations and simplification opportunities.
description_normalized: Final-pass code-review agent that ruthlessly simplifies code via YAGNI principle, removing unnecessary abstraction and redundancy.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-code-simplicity-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Final review pass to ensure code is as simple and minimal as possible.

name: ce-coherence-reviewer
description_raw: Reviews planning documents for internal consistency -- contradictions between sections, terminology drift, structural issues, and ambiguity where readers would diverge. Spawned by the document-review skill.
description_normalized: Document-review agent that catches internal contradictions, terminology drift, and structural inconsistencies within requirements or plan documents.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-coherence-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Reviews planning documents for internal consistency.

name: ce-correctness-reviewer
description_raw: Always-on code-review persona. Reviews code for logic errors, edge cases, state management bugs, error propagation failures, and intent-vs-implementation mismatches.
description_normalized: Always-on code-review agent that mentally executes code to catch logic errors, null propagation, race conditions, and broken error propagation.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-correctness-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Always-on code-review persona. Reviews code for logic errors, edge cases, state management bugs.

name: ce-data-integrity-guardian
description_raw: Reviews database migrations, data models, and persistent data code for safety. Use when checking migration safety, data constraints, transaction boundaries, or privacy compliance.
description_normalized: Specialist agent that audits database migrations and data models for reversibility, NULL handling, ACID compliance, and privacy regulation requirements.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-data-integrity-guardian
frontmatter_yaml.description: Reviews database migrations, data models, and persistent data code for safety.

name: ce-data-migration-expert
description_raw: Validates data migrations, backfills, and production data transformations against reality. Use when PRs involve ID mappings, column renames, enum conversions, or schema changes.
description_normalized: Validates that data migrations match production reality, catches swapped mappings, and ensures concrete SQL verification plans and rollback safety exist.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-data-migration-expert
frontmatter_yaml.description: Validates data migrations and backfills against production reality.

name: ce-data-migrations-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff touches migration files, schema changes, data transformations, or backfill scripts. Reviews code for data integrity and migration safety.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent specializing in migration safety — swapped mappings, irreversible changes, missing backfills, and schema drift during deploy windows.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-data-migrations-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for migration files and schema changes.

name: ce-deployment-verification-agent
description_raw: Produces Go/No-Go deployment checklists with SQL verification queries, rollback procedures, and monitoring plans. Use when PRs touch production data, migrations, or risky data changes.
description_normalized: Produces executable Go/No-Go deployment checklists with SQL verification queries, rollback procedures, and post-deploy monitoring plans for risky data PRs.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-deployment-verification-agent
frontmatter_yaml.description: Produces Go/No-Go deployment checklists for risky data deployments.

name: ce-design-implementation-reviewer
description_raw: Visually compares live UI implementation against Figma designs and provides detailed feedback on discrepancies. Use after writing or modifying HTML/CSS/React components to verify design fidelity.
description_normalized: Captures screenshots of live UI and compares against Figma designs, producing actionable discrepancy feedback on spacing, typography, color, and responsiveness.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-design-implementation-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Visually compares live UI against Figma designs.

name: ce-design-iterator
description_raw: Iteratively refines UI design through N screenshot-analyze-improve cycles. Use PROACTIVELY when design changes aren't coming together after 1-2 attempts, or when user requests iterative refinement.
description_normalized: Runs N screenshot-analyze-improve design iteration cycles, progressively refining UI components toward polished, professional output.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-design-iterator
frontmatter_yaml.description: Iteratively refines UI design through N screenshot-analyze-improve cycles.

name: ce-design-lens-reviewer
description_raw: Reviews planning documents for missing design decisions -- information architecture, interaction states, user flows, and AI slop risk. Uses dimensional rating to identify gaps. Spawned by the document-review skill.
description_normalized: Document-review agent that identifies missing design decisions in plans using a dimensional 0-10 rating system, flagging only scores below 7.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-design-lens-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Reviews planning documents for missing design decisions.

name: ce-dhh-rails-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when Rails diffs introduce architectural choices, abstractions, or frontend patterns that may fight the framework. Reviews code from an opinionated DHH perspective.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that reviews Rails code from DHH's perspective, catching abstractions, JS-world patterns, and convention violations that fight the framework.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-dhh-rails-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona reviewing Rails from DHH's perspective.

name: ce-feasibility-reviewer
description_raw: Evaluates whether proposed technical approaches in planning documents will survive contact with reality -- architecture conflicts, dependency gaps, migration risks, and implementability. Spawned by the document-review skill.
description_normalized: Document-review agent that evaluates whether a plan's technical approach is actually buildable, checking architecture conflicts, dependency gaps, and environmental assumptions.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-feasibility-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Evaluates technical feasibility of planning documents.

name: ce-figma-design-sync
description_raw: Detects and fixes visual differences between a web implementation and its Figma design. Use iteratively when syncing implementation to match Figma specs.
description_normalized: Detects and fixes pixel-level visual differences between web implementations and Figma designs using Figma MCP and agent-browser screenshots.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-figma-design-sync
frontmatter_yaml.description: Detects and fixes visual differences between web implementation and Figma design.

name: ce-framework-docs-researcher
description_raw: Gathers comprehensive documentation and best practices for frameworks, libraries, or dependencies. Use when you need official docs, version-specific constraints, or implementation patterns.
description_normalized: Gathers version-specific framework documentation and best practices via Context7 MCP, ctx7 CLI, or web fallback, synthesized for direct implementation use.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-framework-docs-researcher
frontmatter_yaml.description: Gathers comprehensive documentation and best practices for frameworks.
frontmatter_yaml.tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, WebFetch, WebSearch, mcp__context7__*

name: ce-git-history-analyzer
description_raw: Performs archaeological analysis of git history to trace code evolution, identify contributors, and understand why code patterns exist. Use when you need historical context for code changes.
description_normalized: Performs archaeological git history analysis — tracing file evolution, code origins via blame, and recurring commit patterns to surface why code exists.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-git-history-analyzer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Performs archaeological analysis of git history.

name: ce-issue-intelligence-analyst
description_raw: Fetches and analyzes GitHub issues to surface recurring themes, pain patterns, and severity trends. Use when understanding a project's issue landscape, analyzing bug patterns for ideation, or summarizing what users are reporting.
description_normalized: Transforms raw GitHub issues into theme-level intelligence — recurring pain patterns, severity trends, and investment signals — not ticket-by-ticket summaries.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-issue-intelligence-analyst
frontmatter_yaml.description: Fetches and analyzes GitHub issues to surface recurring themes and pain patterns.

name: ce-julik-frontend-races-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff touches async UI code, Stimulus/Turbo lifecycles, or DOM-timing-sensitive frontend behavior. Reviews code for race conditions and janky UI failure modes.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that catches frontend race conditions — lifecycle cleanup gaps, Turbo/Stimulus timing bugs, concurrent interaction conflicts, and stale async work.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-julik-frontend-races-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for async UI and DOM-timing issues.

name: ce-kieran-python-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff touches Python code. Reviews changes with Kieran's strict bar for Pythonic clarity, type hints, and maintainability.
description_normalized: Conditional Python code-review agent enforcing Pythonic clarity, type hints, testable structure, and explicit error handling.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-kieran-python-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for Python diffs.

name: ce-kieran-rails-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff touches Rails application code. Reviews Rails changes with Kieran's strict bar for clarity, conventions, and maintainability.
description_normalized: Conditional Rails code-review agent that enforces clarity, conventions, and maintainability, prioritizing the next reader six months from now.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-kieran-rails-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for Rails diffs.

name: ce-kieran-typescript-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff touches TypeScript code. Reviews changes with Kieran's strict bar for type safety, clarity, and maintainability.
description_normalized: Conditional TypeScript code-review agent enforcing type safety, clarity, and testable structure with a high bar for existing module complexity.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-kieran-typescript-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for TypeScript diffs.

name: ce-learnings-researcher
description_raw: Searches docs/solutions/ for applicable past learnings via frontmatter metadata (bugs, architecture, design patterns, conventions, workflow learnings). Use before implementing features, making decisions, or starting work in a documented area so institutional knowledge carries forward.
description_normalized: Retrieves applicable past learnings from docs/solutions/ before new work begins, covering bugs, architecture, design patterns, conventions, and workflow discoveries.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-learnings-researcher
frontmatter_yaml.description: Searches docs/solutions/ for applicable past learnings.

name: ce-maintainability-reviewer
description_raw: Always-on code-review persona. Reviews code for premature abstraction, unnecessary indirection, dead code, coupling between unrelated modules, and naming that obscures intent.
description_normalized: Always-on code-review agent that reads from the perspective of the next developer six months from now, catching premature abstraction, dead code, and coupling.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-maintainability-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Always-on code-review persona for maintainability issues.

name: ce-pattern-recognition-specialist
description_raw: Analyzes code for design patterns, anti-patterns, naming conventions, and duplication. Use when checking codebase consistency or verifying new code follows established patterns.
description_normalized: Analyzes code for design patterns, anti-patterns, technical debt markers, and consistency with established conventions.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-pattern-recognition-specialist
frontmatter_yaml.description: Analyzes code for design patterns, anti-patterns, and naming conventions.

name: ce-performance-oracle
description_raw: Analyzes code for performance bottlenecks, algorithmic complexity, database queries, memory usage, and scalability. Use after implementing features or when performance concerns arise.
description_normalized: Analyzes code for performance bottlenecks across algorithmic complexity, database queries, memory allocation, caching, and scalability dimensions.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-performance-oracle
frontmatter_yaml.description: Analyzes code for performance bottlenecks and scalability issues.

name: ce-performance-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff touches database queries, loop-heavy data transforms, caching layers, or I/O-intensive paths. Reviews code for runtime performance and scalability issues.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that catches N+1 queries, unbounded memory growth, missing pagination, hot-path allocations, and blocking I/O in async contexts.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-performance-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for performance-sensitive diffs.

name: ce-pr-comment-resolver
description_raw: Evaluates and resolves one or more related PR review threads -- assesses validity, implements fixes, and returns structured summaries with reply text. Spawned by the resolve-pr-feedback skill.
description_normalized: Evaluates PR review threads for validity, implements fixes, and returns structured summaries with reply text; supports standard and cluster modes.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-pr-comment-resolver
frontmatter_yaml.description: Evaluates and resolves PR review threads.

name: ce-previous-comments-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when reviewing a PR that has existing review comments or review threads. Checks whether prior feedback has been addressed in the current diff.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that verifies prior review feedback has been addressed, serving as institutional memory across the PR review cycle.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-previous-comments-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona checking prior review feedback was addressed.

name: ce-product-lens-reviewer
description_raw: Reviews planning documents as a senior product leader -- challenges premise claims, assesses strategic consequences (trajectory, identity, adoption, opportunity cost), and surfaces goal-work misalignment. Spawned by the document-review skill.
description_normalized: Document-review agent that challenges premises and assesses strategic consequences of requirements or plans from a senior product leader perspective.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-product-lens-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Reviews planning documents as a senior product leader.

name: ce-project-standards-reviewer
description_raw: Always-on code-review persona. Audits changes against the project's own CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md standards -- frontmatter rules, reference inclusion, naming conventions, cross-platform portability, and tool selection policies.
description_normalized: Always-on code-review agent that audits changes against CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md standards files, citing specific rules for every finding.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-project-standards-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Always-on code-review persona auditing against CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md.

name: ce-reliability-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff touches error handling, retries, circuit breakers, timeouts, health checks, background jobs, or async handlers. Reviews code for production reliability and failure modes.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that traces failure propagation paths, catching missing error handling, unbounded retries, missing timeouts, and cascading failure patterns.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-reliability-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for error handling and reliability code.

name: ce-repo-research-analyst
description_raw: Conducts thorough research on repository structure, documentation, conventions, and implementation patterns. Use when onboarding to a new codebase or understanding project conventions.
description_normalized: Conducts systematic repository research across technology stack, documentation conventions, and implementation patterns via configurable scoped phases.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-repo-research-analyst
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conducts thorough research on repository structure and conventions.

name: ce-schema-drift-detector
description_raw: Detects unrelated schema.rb changes in PRs by cross-referencing against included migrations. Use when reviewing PRs with database schema changes.
description_normalized: Detects unrelated schema.rb changes by cross-referencing included migrations, preventing accidental inclusion of changes from other branches.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-schema-drift-detector
frontmatter_yaml.description: Detects unrelated schema.rb changes in PRs.

name: ce-scope-guardian-reviewer
description_raw: Reviews planning documents for scope alignment and unjustified complexity -- challenges unnecessary abstractions, premature frameworks, and scope that exceeds stated goals. Spawned by the document-review skill.
description_normalized: Document-review agent that asks whether plans are right-sized for their goals and whether every abstraction earns its keep.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-scope-guardian-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Reviews planning documents for scope alignment and unjustified complexity.

name: ce-security-lens-reviewer
description_raw: Evaluates planning documents for security gaps at the plan level -- auth/authz assumptions, data exposure risks, API surface vulnerabilities, and missing threat model elements. Spawned by the document-review skill.
description_normalized: Document-review agent that evaluates security gaps in plans before implementation — threat model completeness, auth/authz commitments, and secret handling strategy.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-security-lens-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Evaluates planning documents for security gaps.

name: ce-security-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff touches auth middleware, public endpoints, user input handling, or permission checks. Reviews code for exploitable vulnerabilities.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that thinks like an attacker, tracing injection vectors, auth bypasses, secrets in code, insecure deserialization, and SSRF paths.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-security-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for auth, endpoints, and input handling.

name: ce-security-sentinel
description_raw: Performs security audits for vulnerabilities, input validation, auth/authz, hardcoded secrets, and OWASP compliance. Use when reviewing code for security issues or before deployment.
description_normalized: Performs comprehensive security audits covering input validation, auth/authz, hardcoded secrets, and OWASP compliance using systematic scan protocols.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-security-sentinel
frontmatter_yaml.description: Performs security audits for vulnerabilities and OWASP compliance.

name: ce-session-historian
description_raw: Synthesizes findings from prior coding-agent sessions about the same problem or topic. Receives pre-extracted skeleton/error file paths from a ce-sessions orchestrator and returns prose findings -- investigation journey, what didn't work, key decisions, related context. Not intended for direct dispatch -- use /ce-sessions.
description_normalized: Synthesis-only agent that distills pre-extracted session skeletons into prose about investigation journeys, failed attempts, and key decisions across past coding sessions.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-session-historian
frontmatter_yaml.description: Synthesizes findings from prior coding-agent sessions.

name: ce-slack-researcher
description_raw: Searches Slack for organizational context -- decisions, constraints, and discussions that may not be documented elsewhere. Use when the user explicitly asks to search Slack for context during ideation, planning, or brainstorming.
description_normalized: Searches Slack for organizational decisions, constraints, and discussions to surface undocumented context during ideation, planning, or brainstorming.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-slack-researcher
frontmatter_yaml.description: Searches Slack for organizational context.

name: ce-spec-flow-analyzer
description_raw: Analyzes specifications and feature descriptions for user flow completeness and gap identification. Use when a spec, plan, or feature description needs flow analysis, edge case discovery, or requirements validation.
description_normalized: Analyzes specs and feature descriptions for missing user flows, ambiguous requirements, and edge cases from the end-user perspective before implementation.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-spec-flow-analyzer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Analyzes specifications for user flow completeness and gap identification.

name: ce-swift-ios-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff touches Swift files, SwiftUI/UIKit views, iOS entitlements, privacy manifests, Core Data models, SPM manifests, storyboards/XIBs, or semantic .pbxproj changes. Reviews for SwiftUI correctness, state management, memory safety, Swift concurrency, Core Data threading, and accessibility.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent for Swift/iOS diffs covering SwiftUI state management, memory ownership, concurrency hazards, and Core Data threading.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-swift-ios-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for Swift/iOS diffs.

name: ce-testing-reviewer
description_raw: Always-on code-review persona. Reviews code for test coverage gaps, weak assertions, brittle implementation-coupled tests, and missing edge case coverage.
description_normalized: Always-on code-review agent that evaluates whether tests actually prove the code works — catching coverage gaps, false confidence assertions, and implementation-coupled tests.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-testing-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Always-on code-review persona for test coverage and quality.

name: ce-web-researcher
description_raw: Performs iterative web research and returns structured external grounding. Use when ideating outside the codebase, validating prior art, scanning competitor patterns, finding cross-domain analogies, or fetching market signals.
description_normalized: Performs iterative web research and produces a compact synthesis of prior art, competitor patterns, and market signals as structured external grounding for planning or ideation.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-web-researcher
frontmatter_yaml.description: Performs iterative web research and returns structured external grounding.
